+++
title = "Embed & Plugins"
weight = 24
+++

# Embed & Plugins

Embed files at compile time and extend the language with
compiler plugins.

## Zen C (Embed files as data)

```zc
import "std/io.zc"

fn main() {
    let logo = embed "assets/logo.png";
    println "logo bytes: {logo.len}";

    let shader = embed "shader.glsl" as string;
    println "shader: {shader}";

    let bios = embed "bios.bin" as u8[1024];
    println "bios[0]: {bios[0]}";
}
```

C: No equivalent
C requires external tools (xxd, objcopy, linker scripts) to embed files.
The pattern is:

```c
// Generated by: xxd -i logo.png > logo.h
#include "logo.h"
int main(void) {
    printf("logo bytes: %d\n", logo_png_len);
    return 0;
}
```

## Zen C (Compiler Plugins)

```zc
import plugin "plugins/lisp" as lisp;

fn main() {
    let result = lisp! {
        (defun square (x)
            (* x x))
    };
    println "4 squared: {result(4)}";
}
```

C: No equivalent
C compilers have no plugin system. Alternatives:
- Preprocessor-based DSLs (limited)
- External code generators (separate tooling)
- Lex/yacc for custom language extensions (complex setup)

Key Differences:
- `embed "file"` as `Slice_char` (default), `as string`, `as u8[N]`, `as u8[]`
- `import plugin "..." as name` loads compiler extensions
- `name! { ... }` invokes plugin with custom syntax
- Plugins have LSP hover support for documentation
- C requires external build steps and code generation tools
